triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Policy Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio is significantly above the maximum allowable limit for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to the excessive DTI ratio, which indicates unaffordability.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum DTI ratio for Tier C applicants is 35%. The applicant's DTI ratio of 180.33% far exceeds this limit, suggesting that the loan is unaffordable given their current financial obligations.

triageFlags.title: Loan-to-Value Ratio at Maximum Allowable Limit
triageFlags.description: The LTV ratio is at the maximum allowable limit for the applicant's credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Proceed with caution and consider the high LTV ratio as a significant risk factor.
triageFlags.reasoning: The policy specifies a maximum LTV ratio of 95% for Tier C applicants. While the proposal meets this criterion, being at the maximum limit indicates a lack of financial buffer, increasing the risk of default.

content.reasoning: The analysis identified critical issues with the applicant's financial capacity to manage the proposed loan, as evidenced by a DTI ratio significantly above the policy limit and an LTV ratio at the maximum allowable threshold. These factors collectively indicate a high risk of default.
